proposals.title: UP_TRI × Bear: n=96, 94.7% WR, Tier M → boost candidate (TAKE_SMALL)

claim.what: Promote boost_pattern: signal=UP_TRI, regime=Bear (Tier B / TAKE_SMALL)
claim.expected_effect: Surface as Tier-B boost on cohort matches; observed +31.9pp edge over per-signal baseline

evidence: Wilson 95% lower bound 0.8815 well above Tier M floor — sample size adequate
R-multiple 0.61 below Tier S threshold (1.0); promotion is to Tier M (B/TAKE_SMALL) only, not Tier S (A/TAKE_FULL)
risks: Cohort tier may drift on next archive comparison; demotion auto-fires per §3
R-multiple distribution under D-8 review (Day-6 forced-exit dominance per project_anchor §7 D-8)
R-multiple 0.61 reflects Day-6 forced-exit dominance per project_anchor §7 D-8; if exit policy changes, cohort tier should be re-evaluated

Portfolio currently shows 100% Choppy concentration and DOWN_TRI dominance — when Bear regime returns, this UP_TRI×Bear boost will activate against a portfolio that may rotate quickly; monitor for compounding concentration on regime flip
approval would propagate overlap to mini_scanner_rules.json

proposed_rule_entry.reason: brain_step4: cohort Tier M, n=96, wr=94.7%, edge +31.9pp

decision_metadata.llm_enrichment_rationale: Step 4 tier classification is robust (n=96, Wilson lower 0.8815, +31.9pp edge over UP_TRI baseline) with promotion correctly capped at Tier M/TAKE_SMALL given R-multiple 0.61. Cohort is regime-gated to Bear while current regime is Choppy (stable, 7 days) with zero Bear exposure, so activation is deferred and existing UP_TRI/Choppy concentration is not immediately compounded. Consistent with multiple prior approvals of this exact cohort (2026-04-29 through 2026-05-05) and no trader rejection on record.

proposals.title: UP_TRI × Metal: n=17, 88.2% WR, Tier M → boost candidate (TAKE_SMALL)

claim.what: Promote boost_pattern: signal=UP_TRI, sector=Metal (Tier B / TAKE_SMALL)
claim.expected_effect: Surface as Tier-B boost on cohort matches; observed +25.4pp edge over per-signal baseline

evidence: Wilson 95% lower bound 0.6566 well above Tier M floor — sample size adequate
R-multiple 0.72 below Tier S threshold (1.0); promotion is to Tier M (B/TAKE_SMALL) only, not Tier S (A/TAKE_FULL)
risks: Cohort tier may drift on next archive comparison; demotion auto-fires per §3
R-multiple distribution under D-8 review (Day-6 forced-exit dominance per project_anchor §7 D-8)
Portfolio regime concentration triggered (100% Choppy, 4 concentration flags); monitor whether boost activations on Metal/UP_TRI compound Choppy exposure further
R-multiple 0.72 reflects D-8 forced-exit dominance per project_anchor §7 — true edge may differ once exit policy is revisited
approval would propagate overlap to mini_scanner_rules.json

proposed_rule_entry.reason: brain_step4: cohort Tier M, n=17, wr=88.2%, edge +25.4pp

decision_metadata.llm_enrichment_rationale: Step 4 tier classification is robust (n=17, Wilson lower 0.6566 above Tier M floor, +25.4pp edge over UP_TRI baseline) with promotion correctly capped at Tier M/TAKE_SMALL given R-multiple 0.72. The sector=Metal filter narrows rather than amplifies the existing UP_TRI exposure (3 of 29 open) in a Choppy-concentrated portfolio, and TAKE_SMALL sizing limits incremental concentration. Consistent with multiple prior approvals of this exact cohort (2026-04-29 through 2026-05-05) with no trader rejection on record.

proposals.title: Exposure concentration: by_regime + by_signal_type×sector + by_signal_type = Choppy (100%); DOWN_TRI×Choppy×Auto (27.59%), DOWN_TRI×Choppy×IT (20.69%); DOWN_TRI 89.7% (100.0%)

claim.what: Concentration risk: by_regime + by_signal_type×sector + by_signal_type = Choppy (100%); DOWN_TRI×Choppy×Auto (27.59%), DOWN_TRI×Choppy×IT (20.69%); DOWN_TRI 89.7%; recommended action: Pause new entries in DOWN_TRI×Auto and DOWN_TRI×IT until concentration drops via natural exits (Day 6). Block additional DOWN_TRI signals broadly until DOWN_TRI share falls below 0.85. Do not add to Choppy-regime positions until a regime cohort reaches Tier M.
claim.expected_effect: Trader awareness of tail risk: If regime flips from Choppy to Bull or a sector-rotation rally hits Auto/IT simultaneously, 89.7% of the book takes correlated drawdown with no validated cohort edge to support the directional bet — Auto and IT shorts unwinding together would impact ~48% of positions in a single session.

evidence: All concentration cells match only Candidate-tier cohort (regime=Choppy__signal_type=DOWN_TRI, n=6) — unvalidated edge
Combined Auto+IT short cluster = 14/29 = 48.3% of book in two sectors
risks: Recommendation may be over-cautious; concentration may be edge-validated
Trader may already be aware; informational only

proposals.reversibility: Informational warning; no system mutation. Suppresses on next run if concentration drops below threshold.

decision_metadata.recommended_action: Pause new entries in DOWN_TRI×Auto and DOWN_TRI×IT until concentration drops via natural exits (Day 6). Block additional DOWN_TRI signals broadly until DOWN_TRI share falls below 0.85. Do not add to Choppy-regime positions until a regime cohort reaches Tier M.
decision_metadata.tail_risk_scenario: If regime flips from Choppy to Bull or a sector-rotation rally hits Auto/IT simultaneously, 89.7% of the book takes correlated drawdown with no validated cohort edge to support the directional bet — Auto and IT shorts unwinding together would impact ~48% of positions in a single session.
decision_metadata.llm_rationale: Portfolio is 100% Choppy regime (above 0.80 threshold) AND two cells breach the 20% cell threshold: DOWN_TRI×Choppy×Auto at 27.59% and DOWN_TRI×Choppy×IT at 20.69%. All concentration cells map only to Candidate-tier cohort (n=6), so this is unvalidated edge — nearly half the book sits in two sector shorts with no statistical justification, and DOWN_TRI dominates 26/29 = 89.7% of signals, exceeding the 0.85 single-signal threshold.
